text.title: Freelancer Niche Positioning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Freelancer Niche Positioning. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Positioning MUST follow 'I [verb X] for [buyer Y] so they [measurable outcome Z]'. All three slots filled.
rule.rationale: Format forces specificity; unfilled slots are the most common failure mode.

rule.statement: Outcome Z MUST be measurable (a unit + direction): hours saved, % conversion, $ recovered, days reduced. Qualitative outcomes ('grow', 'improve') are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Numeric outcomes force the buyer to picture a concrete result.

rule.statement: Typing X+Y into Google / LinkedIn / Upwork MUST find you in the top 5 results within 30 days of positioning publish.
rule.rationale: If the buyer searches the phrase and doesn't see you, the positioning is invisible.

rule.statement: ≥3 of 5 peers MUST paraphrase your one-liner back unprompted within 30 days. 'I think you do X-ish' fails; specifics required.
rule.rationale: Peer recall is a leading indicator of buyer recall; if peers don't remember, buyers won't.

rule.statement: ≥1 buyer in the last 30 days MUST repeat outcome Z back during discovery without prompting.
rule.rationale: Buyer paraphrase = positioning landed in the segment.
